text: The patient is positioned in a right decubitus position for the nephrectomy. A transperitoneal approach is used to establish pneumoperitoneum with a balloon port. Three 12mm ports and one 5mm port are inserted. Adhesions are divided, and the peritoneum is incised along the line of Toldt, allowing medial mobilization of the left colon.

The surgeon uses blunt dissection to identify and dissect the left ureter and gonadal vessels distally. These structures are dissected en bloc and lifted anteriorly off the psoas muscle. The dissection proceeds cranially towards the hilum, where the left renal vein and artery are identified and circumferentially mobilized.

The surgeon isolates the left renal artery and loops a vessel loop around it twice, preparing it as a selective tourniquet. Girota's fascia is entered, and the renal surface is defatted, revealing a significant amount of perinephric fat.

The surgeon uses laparoscopic intraoperative ultrasound to delineate the tumour's extent. The renal artery is clamped with a vessel loop, and a scored line of excision is made on the renal capsule using a diathermy probe. The tumour is excised with cold shears. Rhinoraphy is performed using a two-layer technique with V-locked barbed sutures and haemlock clips, facilitating knotless suturing and minimizing warm ischemic time.

The surgeon secures the sutures with single haemlock clips and applies Floseal haemostatic matrix to the renal parenchyma for haemostasis and mechanical tamponade. Interrupted V-lock sutures are used to close the renal capsule and perinephric fat. The arterial clamp is released, and the specimen is extracted through an extended left iliac fossa port incision.
